PROD EVENTFUL MY-01 DATES 11–19 AUG 2026 LOC KUALA LUMPUR + KLANG VALLEY CREW @EVENTFULSG

8 days.
KL. Every day mapped.

The daily shoot plan for the Malaysia expansion. Same rule every day: one cultural spot, one local food spot, one business meeting — clustered by zone so you never cross the Klang Valley twice. Every spot below is researched, priced, and queue-checked.

D1 · ARRIVED2 · CHEESED3 · PJD4 · HERITAGED5 · EVENT NIGHTD6 · BATU CAVESD7 · CHINATOWND8 · WRAPRULES
DAY 1/8Tue 11 AugKLCC → Bukit Bintang

Land, declare it, shoot the icon

Light day on purpose — arrival admin, dinner where locals actually eat, then the most cinematic opener in the city. Tonight's upload is the announcement video, and it already has a local-vs-tourist angle built in.

14:00Logistics

KLIA → check-in, Bukit Bintang base

✈️ KLIA Ekspres to KL Sentral 28 min · then MRT/Grab to hotel

Base yourself on the Bukit Bintang–KL Sentral corridor: 6 of the 8 days route through the city core. Sort eSIM, Grab payment and a Touch 'n Go eWallet before anything else — half the food spots this week are wallet-or-cash only.

RM55 KLIA EkspresRM300 cash, small notes

Shoot: the airport walk + first Grab ride = b-roll for the Day 1 hook. Record the hook line while energy is high.

17:00Food🟢 locals' pick

Pudu Wai Sek Kai (Glutton Street) — dinner with the locals

📍 Jalan Sayur, off Jalan Pudu · 🚇 LRT Pudu, 200 m · ⏰ stalls fire up ~16:30–midnight · map

📷 real pics: Maps · IG · TikTok

One lane, ~20 hawker stalls, 60+ years of history, and almost no tourists — this is where KL people actually eat at night. Old-school Cantonese street plates at single-serving prices. Two streets over, Chen Tiang Cake House (since 1961) just trended on your own radar via foodie.my.

RM2.50–7 a dish60+ years

Shoot: this is the "locals sent me here" segment — grimy, fluorescent, zero styling, all flavour. It cuts perfectly against the next stop.

19:00Culture🟢 locals love it too

KLCC Park → Petronas Towers at blue hour

📍 KLCC Park, Jalan Ampang · 🚇 LRT KLCC · map

📷 real pics: Maps · IG · TikTok

Local check: not just a tourist stop — the park is where KL families spend their evenings, and the free fountain show pulls a local crowd nightly.

The definitive KL establishing shot. Arrive at golden hour, get the towers as the sky turns, then stay for the Lake Symphony fountain show on the park side.

452 m twin towersFountain shows ~20:00

Shoot: talking-head with towers behind you — this is the "day 1 of expanding my media business into Malaysia" frame. Free, no tickets needed for the park side.

21:00Culture🟠 tourist icon

Jalan Alor — the icon walk (snack, don't feast)

📍 Jalan Alor, Bukit Bintang · 🚶 8 min from Bukit Bintang MRT · map

📷 real pics: Maps · IG · TikTok

Local check: honest verdict — KL locals call it overpriced (a RM50 plate here is RM15–20 in a neighbourhood spot) and mostly leave it to tourists. So you already ate at Pudu; you're here for the neon walking shot and one plate of Wong Ah Wah's charcoal wings, which even the locals concede.

The lantern-lit street IS the classic KL night frame — and "tourists eat here, locals eat 10 minutes away, I did both" is your Day 1 story beat.

Wings ~RM3.90 eachRM50 vs RM15 — the trap math

Shoot: lantern-lit walking shot down the middle + the wing bite. Say the price math to camera — that's the local-cred moment. Peak atmosphere after 21:00.

🎬 Tonight's upload"This is day 1 of expanding my media business into Malaysia — and I'm gonna vlog every single part of it." Full script in the repo plan. Cliffhanger: "tomorrow I'm meeting a Malaysian artisan cheesemaker."
DAY 2/8Wed 12 AugNW · Damansara–Kepong

The cheesemaker day

Tightest cluster of the trip — all three stops within 15 minutes of each other in the northwest. Meeting #1: Dexter.

07:45Food🟢 local legend

Village Park Restaurant — nasi lemak ayam goreng

📍 5, Jalan SS 21/37, Damansara Utama, PJ · ⏰ 06:30–17:30 daily · map

📷 real pics: Maps · IG · TikTok

Local check: the queue is Malaysians — this is the most argued-about nasi lemak in the Klang Valley, and losing the argument still means a great plate.

The most famous nasi lemak in the Klang Valley — the fried chicken is spiced, rested, and the queue is three lines deep by mid-morning (one to enter, one to sit, one to pay). Going at open is the move.

~RM15 with ayam goreng45-min queue after 09:00

Shoot: the queue IS the hook — "locals queue 45 minutes for this plate." Order extra fried chicken, film the chop.

10:30Business

Dexter — D Artisan Cheese Ventures

📍 26, Jalan Gangsa SD5/3b, Bandar Sri Damansara · 🚗 ~15 min from Village Park · map

Meeting #1, at his facility. A Malaysian artisan cheesemaker is both strong vlog material and the prototype of the F&B brand client Eventful MY would serve. Ask how MY brands buy marketing today, and what he'd honestly pay for.

Meeting 1 of 6

Ask first, then film: the make-room b-roll. Close with "who are 2 other people I should meet before the 19th?"

14:30Culture🟢 locals' escape

FRIM Kepong — rainforest + Forest Skywalk

📍 Forest Research Institute Malaysia, Kepong · 🚗 ~10 min from Dexter · map

📷 real pics: Maps · IG · TikTok

Local check: barely on tourist lists — it's where KL people jog, hike and cool off on weekends.

A 545-hectare century-old research rainforest inside the city limits, with a steel skywalk up in the canopy. The "KL is a jungle city, literally" segment — total contrast to yesterday's towers.

~RM40 skywalk (foreigner)30 m above ground

Book the skywalk slot in advance — limited daily capacity, and confirm it's open that week. Backup if closed: Desa ParkCity lakeside golden hour, 10 min away.

🎬 Tonight's upload"There's a guy making artisan cheese in a KL suburb — day 2 of expanding into Malaysia." Cliffhanger: "tomorrow, kombucha."
DAY 3/8Thu 13 AugPetaling Jaya–Subang

The startup day (PJ)

Out to PJ for Wonderbrew, then eat where Subang students eat, then the manufactured-city story. Meeting #2: Joseph.

11:00Business

Joseph — Wonderbrew taproom

📍 No. 73, Jalan PJS 11/7, Bandar Sunway (or Bukit Utama outlet — his pick) · map

Meeting #2. A homegrown kombucha brand that grew from startup to Jaya Grocer shelves — Joseph knows where Malaysian consumer attention actually converts. Tasting flight on camera is the easiest content of the week.

Meeting 2 of 6

Ask: which pages/KOLs actually moved product, and what a launch campaign costs in MY vs what it'd cost in SG.

13:30Food🟢 students' turf

SS15 Subang Jaya — cendol + rojak street

📍 Jalan SS 15/4, Subang Jaya · 🚗 10 min from taproom · 🚇 LRT SS15 · map

📷 real pics: Maps · IG · TikTok

Local check: SS15 is where Klang Valley students eat; people genuinely cross town for this cendol corner.

SS15 is the student-food capital of the Klang Valley, and its cendol corner is the famous one — shaved ice, gula melaka, pandan noodles, coconut milk. The post-meeting cooldown in 33° heat.

~RM6 a bowl

Shoot: the gula melaka pour in slow-mo. Add a plate of rojak for the table shot.

16:30Culture🟡 local-built playground

Sunway City — the town Malaysia built from a mine

📍 Sunway Pyramid / Sunway Lagoon rim · 🚗 5 min from SS15 · map

📷 real pics: Maps · IG · TikTok

Local check: not heritage — but the crowd here is overwhelmingly Malaysian. This is where the Klang Valley takes its own family weekend.

A disused tin-mining wasteland turned into an entire private city — pyramid mall, lagoon, university, its own elevated bus line. Modern-Malaysia culture beat: this is the ambition side of the country, and it pairs against tomorrow's heritage day.

Built on 800 acres of ex-mine

Shoot: the pyramid + lion facade at dusk, then the skyline from the canopy walk bridge if time allows.

🎬 Tonight's upload"A kombucha founder just told me exactly how Malaysian brands spend money." Cliffhanger: "tomorrow morning, a 3-generation kopitiam breakfast — and it's a business meeting."
DAY 4/8Fri 14 AugS · Kuchai Lama–Seputeh–Brickfields

The heritage day

South corridor, chronological and 10 minutes apart: kopitiam breakfast meeting, the grandest Chinese temple in Southeast Asia, then banana leaf in Little India. Meeting #3: Guzai.

08:30BusinessFood🟢 neighbourhood regulars

Guzai Kopitiam — breakfast meeting (Laraine's intro)

📍 Kuchai Lama · 🚇 MRT Kuchai · map

Meeting #3 doubles as the food slot: a Nanyang heritage kopitiam run by the family you're meeting. Kopi, kaya toast, soft-boiled eggs — and the operator's-eye view of what a viral feature actually does to a small F&B business. That answer is Eventful MY's product spec.

Meeting 3 of 6Breakfast ~RM12

Warm intro chain (Laraine → her bf's sister) — keep it genuine, feature them properly, no hard sell.

11:00Culture🟢 locals worship here

Thean Hou Temple

📍 65, Persiaran Endah, Seputeh · ⏰ 08:00–20:00 daily, free · 🚗 10 min from Guzai · map

📷 real pics: Maps · IG · TikTok

Local check: an active temple first — locals pray, register marriages, and pack it out at CNY. The lantern terraces became an IG darling second.

Six-tiered temple to the sea goddess Mazu, one of the largest in Southeast Asia — red columns, hundreds of hanging lanterns, and a hilltop terrace with a clean skyline view. The lantern corridors are the most Instagrammed frame in KL for a reason.

6 tiersFree entry

Shoot: lantern ceiling walk-through + skyline from the upper deck. Midweek morning = thin crowds.

13:30Food🟢 office-crowd ritual

Vishal Food & Catering — banana leaf, Brickfields

📍 22, Jalan Scott, Brickfields · 🚇 KL Sentral / Monorail Tun Sambanthan · map

📷 real pics: Maps · IG · TikTok

Local check: banana leaf is a KL lunch institution and Brickfields is its home turf — Vishal's lunch crowd is local office workers, not tour groups.

Brickfields is KL's Little India, and Vishal is its benchmark banana leaf: rice on leaf, three vegetables, curries poured until you say stop, papadum. Eat with your hand, fold the leaf toward you when done.

~RM15–20 with sides

Shoot: the curry pour overhead + the leaf-folding etiquette explainer. Walk Jalan Tun Sambanthan's arch after for the Little India b-roll.

🎬 Tonight's upload"A kopitiam owner told me what going viral actually does to a small business." Cliffhanger: "tomorrow: the ticketing platform behind Malaysia's live events — and we're going to a show."
DAY 5/8Sat 15 AugCity core · Merdeka–Ampang

Event night (the hero day)

Colonial core by day, Gimmetix by afternoon, a live show by night, mamak at midnight. This is the episode that shows what Eventful MY would actually do. Meeting #4: Darrell.

10:00Culture🟢 national icon

Merdeka Square + Sultan Abdul Samad Building

📍 Dataran Merdeka · 🚇 LRT Masjid Jamek · map

📷 real pics: Maps · IG · TikTok

Local check: mid-August is Merdeka season — flags and buntings go up citywide ahead of 31 Aug, so the square is at its most alive exactly when you're there.

Where the Union Jack came down and the Malaysian flag first rose in 1957, in front of the copper-domed 1897 Sultan Abdul Samad Building. Walk two minutes to Masjid Jamek at the meeting point of the two rivers that named Kuala Lumpur — "muddy confluence."

95 m flagpoleBuilt 1897

Shoot: the independence story to camera in one take on the square — it's the "why Malaysia" narrative beat of the series.

15:00Business

Darrell — Gimmetix, then cover one of their live events

📍 Venue: his pick · 🎫 gimmetix.com — pick the Sat 15 Aug event together

Meeting #4, the top-priority one: Gimmetix has the MY events pipeline, Eventful has the content engine. Pitch coverage-for-access + affiliate links, then walk the talk by shooting one of their events that same night — the meeting becomes the proof of concept in a single day.

Meeting 4 of 6Show at ~20:00

Before the trip: ask Darrell which event is running that Saturday and get press/photo access sorted.

23:30Food🟢 the local institution

Pelita Nasi Kandar, Jalan Ampang — mamak supper

📍 Jalan Ampang (24h) · post-show wind-down · map

📷 real pics: Maps · IG · TikTok

Local check: the mamak is Malaysia's actual nightlife — this is where everyone ends up after everything, and Pelita's Ampang branch runs till late.

The mamak is Malaysia's real nightlife: fluorescent lights, football on TV, roti canai flipped to order and teh tarik pulled a metre through the air at 1am. Non-negotiable KL experience, and the honest debrief setting for the day's episode.

Roti canai ~RM2Teh tarik ~RM3Open 24h

Shoot: the tarik pull in slow-mo + to-camera event recap over roti. This is the episode's closing scene.

🎬 Tonight's upload"We covered a Malaysian live show with the ticketing platform that runs them." Cliffhanger: "tomorrow, 272 rainbow steps at 7am."
DAY 6/8Sun 16 AugN · Batu Caves–Kampung Baru

The north day

Sacred limestone at sunrise, the OG nasi lemak at noon, village-vs-skyline at golden hour. Flex slot for Melly. Meeting #5 (tentative).

07:00Culture🟡 sacred + touristy

Batu Caves at opening

📍 Gombak · ⏰ 07:00–21:00, free · 🚆 KTM to Batu Caves station or 20-min Grab · map

📷 real pics: Maps · IG · TikTok

Local check: a working Hindu temple that happens to be a tourist magnet — at 7am it's worshippers and monkeys; by 10 it's tour buses. Locals' own advice: go at open.

A 400-million-year-old limestone cave temple behind a 42.7 m golden Murugan statue and the rainbow staircase. At 7am you get soft light, cool air and nearly empty steps; by 10 it's crowds, heat and monkeys with agendas.

272 steps42.7 m statueFree

Dress code: knees covered (sarong rental on site). Hold your phone tight — the macaques really do snatch.

11:30Food🟢 the OG since 1963

Nasi Lemak Wanjo, Kampung Baru

📍 8, Jalan Raja Muda Musa, Kampung Baru · 🚇 LRT Kampung Baru, 250 m walk · map

📷 real pics: Maps · IG · TikTok

Local check: three generations, royalty has eaten here, 11k+ reviews mostly Malaysian — and the eternal "sambal too sweet?" debate in the comments is itself local culture.

The OG. A family stall since 1963, three generations in, visited by royalty, 11k+ reviews. The coconut rice is the point — aromatic enough to eat on its own. Get the ayam goreng berempah and the rendang.

Since 1963~RM12–15 a plate

Compare on camera: Wanjo vs Wednesday's Village Park — "old guard vs new king" is a ready-made debate video for the comments.

15:00BusinessFlex

Melly (tentative) → then Kampung Baru golden-hour walk

📍 Meeting TBC · 🌇 Walk: Jalan Raja Muda Musa → Sultan Sulaiman Club area

If Melly confirms, this is the slot. Either way, end the day walking Kampung Baru — a 1900s Malay village of timber stilt houses holding out against the KLCC skyline directly behind it. The single best visual metaphor for this whole series.

Meeting 5 of 6 · confirm by 8 Aug

If unconfirmed: radar-reactive slot — pull the morning's 8am scan and swap in whatever's blowing up within 20 minutes of the zone.

🎬 Tonight's upload"272 steps, the best nasi lemak argument in KL, and a village that refuses to sell." Cliffhanger: "tomorrow, the oldest kopitiam queue in Chinatown."
DAY 7/8Mon 17 AugChinatown–Merdeka 118

The Chinatown day

All on foot: 1956 kopitiam, restored laneways, a bookstore in an old cinema, the world's second-tallest tower. Flex slot for Daniel. Meeting #6 (tentative).

07:40Food🟢 heritage queue

Ho Kow Hainam Kopitiam

📍 1, Jalan Balai Polis, Chinatown · ⏰ opens 07:30, queue from open · 🚇 MRT Pasar Seni · map

📷 real pics: Maps · IG · TikTok

Local check: the 7:30am queue is KL regulars alongside visitors — 70 years of the same charcoal kaya toast earns that mix.

Hainanese breakfast institution since 1956: charcoal-toasted bread with pandan kaya, soft-boiled eggs, kopi O. The queue snakes from the moment doors open — be there ten minutes early and you're first seating.

Since 1956Breakfast ~RM10–15

Shoot: the egg-crack over toast + kopi pour at the marble table. Retro interior does half the work.

09:30Culture🟡 mixed crowd

Kwai Chai Hong → Petaling Street → REXKL

📍 Lorong Panggung → Jalan Petaling → REXKL, Jalan Sultan · all walking · map

📷 real pics: Maps · IG · TikTok

Local check: Petaling Street itself is a tourist gauntlet, but Kwai Chai Hong and REXKL are local creative-scene turf. Dinner tonight: the original Kim Lian Kee on Petaling Street (since 1927 — the Hutong outlet is the mall version), or Wawa Fish, the loft-style Guizhou spot your own radar flagged, open till 2am.

Chinatown in three acts: Kwai Chai Hong's restored 1960s laneway murals, the Petaling Street market gauntlet, then REXKL — a 1947 cinema reborn as a creative hub with BookXcess's floor-to-ceiling book hall inside. That bookstore shot is one of the most viral frames in KL.

1947 cinemaFree to wander

Coffee stop: the specialty cafes around Jalan Sultan are where KL's creative scene actually sits on a Monday.

15:00BusinessFlex

Daniel Cerventus (tentative) → Merdeka 118 at dusk

📍 Coffee, Chinatown side · then Stadium Merdeka vantage for the tower

If it's the Daniel of KL's startup-community world, this is the ecosystem meeting — one good conversation here is ten warm intros. Then close the day under Merdeka 118: at 678.9 m it's the second-tallest building on Earth, best framed from Stadium Merdeka where independence was declared.

Meeting 6 of 6 · confirm by 8 Aug678.9 m

If unconfirmed: radar-reactive slot, same rule as Day 6.

🎬 Tonight's upload"KL's oldest breakfast and its newest supertall in one day — plus what the startup scene told me." Cliffhanger: "tomorrow I answer the question: is Eventful expanding into Malaysia?"
DAY 8/8Tue 18 AugPerdana–Bukit Bintang

The decision day

Last cultural heavyweight, last great meal, then the payoff video — the honest yes/no on the expansion, recapped over everything filmed this week.

09:30Culture🟢 local pride

Islamic Arts Museum + Masjid Negara

📍 Jalan Lembah, Perdana Gardens · ⏰ 09:30–18:00 daily · map

📷 real pics: Maps · IG · TikTok

Local check: world-class and genuinely beloved at home — school trips, design students, quiet regulars. Malaysians rate it above most "tourist" stops.

Southeast Asia's largest Islamic arts collection — twelve galleries under inverted tiled domes, plus the miniature architecture hall. Walk five minutes to Masjid Negara, the national mosque with its 16-point folded-umbrella roof (robes provided, free, outside prayer times).

RM20 entry12 galleries

Shoot: the dome interiors and geometry close-ups — the calmest, most cinematic b-roll of the trip for the decision-video voiceover.

13:00Food🟡 mixed verdict

Hutong, Lot 10 — heritage hawker hall

📍 Lot 10 basement, Bukit Bintang · 🚇 MRT Bukit Bintang · map

📷 real pics: Maps · IG · TikTok

Local check: locals are split — convenient air-conditioned heritage sampler vs "mall prices, eat at the originals." Fair trade on a wrap day when you need aircon and an edit session; you had the original Kim Lian Kee last night anyway.

A curated basement of KL's multi-generation hawker legends. Go for Kim Lian Kee — the stall credited with inventing KL-style Hokkien mee around 1927: thick noodles, dark soy, pork lard crisps, wok hei for days.

Hokkien mee ~RM13Since 1927

Air-conditioned — the one guilt-free indoor food stop, perfect for a midday card-offload and edit session after.

18:00Business

The decision video — KLCC Park, golden hour

📍 Same spot as Day 1, on purpose · overflow meeting slot 15:00 if intros stacked up

Bookend the series where it started. One line per meeting, the honest math — what people said they'd pay for, what the radar shows daily — and the answer, said plainly on camera: is Eventful expanding into Malaysia? Commit. This is the video that gets clipped.

6 meetings recapped8/8 streak

Wed 19: fly home. Record the airport outro before security — "so… here's what happens next."

🎬 Tonight's uploadThe payoff: "Day 8/8 — the decision." Recap montage over Islamic Arts b-roll, answer delivered at KLCC with the towers behind you, same frame as Day 1.

Standing rules, every day

08:00 · Radar

Check the morning scan

The radar scans 21 KL pages at 8:00 (growth-rate rescan 8:45). Anything ≥4× outperformance within 20 min of the day's zone gets swapped in or added as a fourth stop — "my radar says this is going viral in KL today, so we're going."

Shoot · Checklist

Six shots minimum

Morning hook to camera · walking-in shot at each stop · food close-up + first bite · one insight to camera right after the meeting · 2 min of street b-roll · cliffhanger line before bed. Rough-cut and post the same night.

Meetings · Closers

Same three closers

"Who are 2 other people I should meet before the 19th?" · "Can I film a bit of this?" (asked before the camera comes out) · a next step with a date attached, never "let's keep in touch."

House rule

No number, no post

Same rule as the caption cheat sheet: every spot and every caption carries a number — a price in RM, a queue time, a founding year, a height. It's why every card on this page has one.

Weather

August = afternoon storms

Sequence outdoor spots before 15:00 where possible. Wet-weather swaps: REXKL, Islamic Arts Museum, Hutong, Suria KLCC — all indoor, all already on the plan.

Money

Cash + wallet reality

RM300 in small notes for kopitiams, Wanjo and Kampung Baru; Touch 'n Go eWallet for nearly everything else. Cards are for malls only. Grab across the trip ≈ RM250–350 total.

EVENTFUL MY-01 · 11–19 AUG 2026 · CALL SHEET v1 · Built for the Eventful team 🧡
Aerial imagery © Google. Local verdicts researched Jul 2026 (SethLui, Johor Kaki, OpenRice, Coconuts KL, Tripadvisor locals' threads + the Eventful radar). Hours and prices checked Jul 2026 — reconfirm the week before (FRIM Skywalk booking, Gimmetix event pick, Melly + Daniel by 8 Aug). Meeting briefs and scripts live in kl-trip-plan.md.
